Box plot[edit]
Box plot is a method for graphically depicting groups of numerical data 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  The maximum and minimum values are represented by the lines, and the interquartile range (IQR) represent 25–75% of the data 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Outliers may be plotted as circles 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

Correlation coefficients[edit]
Although correlations between two different kinds of data could be inferred by graphs, such as scatter plot, it is necessary validate this though numerical information 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  For this reason, correlation coefficients are required 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  They provide a numerical value that reflects the strength of an association 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [11]

Pearson correlation coefficient[edit]
Scatter diagram that demonstrates the Pearson correlation for different values of ρ 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Pearson correlation coefficient is a measure of association between two variables, X and Y 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  This coefficient, usually represented by ρ (rho) for the population and r for the sample, assumes values between −1 and 1, where ρ  = 1 represents a perfect positive correlation, ρ = −1 represents a perfect negative correlation, and ρ = 0 is no linear correlation 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence: [11]
Inferential statistics[edit]
Main article: Statistical inference
It is used to make inferences[16] about an unknown population, by estimation and/or hypothesis testing 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In other words, it is desirable to obtain parameters to describe the population of interest, but since the data is limited, it is necessary to make use of a representative sample in order to estimate them 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  With that, it is possible to test previously defined hypotheses and apply the conclusions to the entire population 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The  standard error of the mean is a measure of variability that is crucial to do inferences 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [6]

Hypothesis testing
Hypothesis testing is essential to make inferences about populations aiming to answer research questions, as settled in "Research planning" section 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Authors defined four steps to be set:[6]

The hypothesis to be tested: as stated earlier, we have to work with the definition of a null hypothesis (H0), that is going to be tested, and an alternative hypothesis 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  But they must be defined before the experiment implementation 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Significance level and decision rule: A decision rule depends on the level of significance, or in other words, the acceptable error rate (α) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is easier to think that we define a critical value that determines the statistical significance when a test statistic is compared with it 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  So, α also has to be predefined before the experiment 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Experiment and statistical analysis: This is when the experiment is really implemented following the appropriate experimental design, data is collected and the more suitable statistical tests are evaluated 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Inference: Is made when the null hypothesis is rejected or not rejected, based on the evidence that the comparison of p-values and α brings 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is pointed that the failure to reject H0 just means that there is not enough evidence to support its rejection, but not that this hypothesis is true 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Confidence intervals
A confidence interval is a range of values that can contain the true real parameter value in given a certain level of confidence 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The first step is to estimate the best-unbiased estimate of the population parameter 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The upper value of the interval is obtained by the sum of this estimate with the multiplication between the standard error of the mean and the confidence level 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The calculation of lower value is similar, but instead of a sum, a subtraction must be applied 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [6]

Statistical considerations[edit]
Power and statistical error[edit]
When testing a hypothesis, there are two types of statistic errors possible: Type I error and Type II error 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence: 

The type I error or false positive is the incorrect rejection of a true null hypothesis
The type II error or false negative is the failure to reject a false null hypothesis 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
The significance level denoted by α is the type I error rate and should be chosen before performing the test 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The type II error rate is denoted by β and statistical power of the test is 1 − β 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

p-value[edit]
The p-value is the probability of obtaining results as extreme as or more extreme than those observed, assuming the null hypothesis (H0) is true 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  It is also called the calculated probability 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is common to confuse the p-value with the significance level (α), but, the α is a predefined threshold for calling significant results 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  If p is less than α, the null hypothesis (H0) is rejected 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [17]

Multiple testing[edit]
In multiple tests of the same hypothesis, the probability of the occurrence of false positives (familywise error rate) increase and a strategy is needed to account for this occurrence 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  This is commonly achieved by using a more stringent threshold to reject null hypotheses 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The Bonferroni correction defines an acceptable global significance level, denoted by α* and each test is individually compared with a value of α =  α*/m 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  This ensures that the familywise error rate in all m tests, is less than or equal to α* 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  When m is large, the Bonferroni correction may be overly conservative 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  An alternative to the Bonferroni correction is to control the false discovery rate (FDR) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The FDR controls the expected proportion of the rejected null hypotheses (the so-called discoveries) that are false (incorrect rejections) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  This procedure ensures that, for independent tests, the false discovery rate is at most q* 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Thus, the FDR is less conservative than the Bonferroni correction and have more power, at the cost of more false positives 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [18]

Mis-specification and robustness checks[edit]
The main hypothesis being tested (e 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: g 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: , no association between treatments and outcomes) is often accompanied by other technical assumptions (e 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: g 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: , about the form of the probability distribution of the outcomes) that are also part of the null hypothesis 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  When the technical assumptions are violated in practice, then the null may be frequently rejected even if the main hypothesis is true 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Such rejections are said to be due to model mis-specification 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [19] Verifying whether the outcome of a statistical test does not change when the technical assumptions are slightly altered (so-called robustness checks) is the main way of combating mis-specification 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

Model selection criteria[edit]
Model criteria selection will select or model that more approximate true model 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The Akaike's Information Criterion (AIC) and The Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) are examples of asymptotically efficient criteria 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

Developments and big data[edit]
This section needs additional citations for verification 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Please help improve this article by adding citations to reliable sources in this section 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Unsourced material may be challenged and removed 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  (December 2016) (Learn how and when to remove this message)
Recent developments have made a large impact on biostatistics 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Two important changes have been the ability to collect data on a high-throughput scale, and the ability to perform much more complex analysis using computational techniques 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  This comes from the development in areas as sequencing technologies, Bioinformatics and Machine learning (Machine learning in bioinformatics) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

Use in high-throughput data[edit]
New biomedical technologies like microarrays, next-generation sequencers (for genomics) and mass spectrometry (for proteomics) generate enormous amounts of data, allowing many tests to be performed simultaneously 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence: [20] Careful analysis with biostatistical methods is required to separate the signal from the noise 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence:  For example, a microarray could be used to measure many thousands of genes simultaneously, determining which of them have different expression in diseased cells compared to normal cells 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  However, only a fraction of genes will be differentially expressed 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence: [21]
Multicollinearity often occurs in high-throughput biostatistical settings 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Due to high intercorrelation between the predictors (such as gene expression levels), the information of one predictor might be contained in another one 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It could be that only 5% of the predictors are responsible for 90% of the variability of the response 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  In such a case, one could apply the biostatistical technique of dimension reduction (for example via principal component analysis) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Classical statistical techniques like linear or logistic regression and linear discriminant analysis do not work well for high dimensional data (i 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: e 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  when the number of observations n is smaller than the number of features or predictors p: n < p) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  As a matter of fact, one can get quite high R2-values despite very low predictive power of the statistical model 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  These classical statistical techniques (esp 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  least squares linear regression) were developed for low dimensional data (i 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: e 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  where the number of observations n is much larger than the number of predictors p: n >> p) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In cases of high dimensionality, one should always consider an independent validation test set and the corresponding residual sum of squares (RSS) and R2 of the validation test set, not those of the training set 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Often, it is useful to pool information from multiple predictors together 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  For example, Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) considers the perturbation of whole (functionally related) gene sets rather than of single genes 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [22] These gene sets might be known biochemical pathways or otherwise functionally related genes 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The advantage of this approach is that it is more robust: It is more likely that a single gene is found to be falsely perturbed than it is that a whole pathway is falsely perturbed 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Furthermore, one can integrate the accumulated knowledge about biochemical pathways (like the JAK-STAT signaling pathway) using this approach 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 

Bioinformatics advances in databases, data mining, and biological interpretation[edit]
The development of biological databases enables storage and management of biological data with the possibility of ensuring access for users around the world 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  They are useful for researchers depositing data, retrieve information and files (raw or processed) originated from other experiments or indexing scientific articles, as PubMed 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Another possibility is search for the desired term (a gene, a protein, a disease, an organism, and so on) and check all results related to this search 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  There are databases dedicated to SNPs (dbSNP), the knowledge on genes characterization and their pathways (KEGG) and the description of gene function classifying it by cellular component, molecular function and biological process (Gene Ontology) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [23] In addition to databases that contain specific molecular information, there are others that are ample in the sense that they store information about an organism or group of organisms 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  As an example of a database directed towards just one organism, but that contains much data about it, is the Arabidopsis thaliana genetic and molecular database – TAIR 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [24] Phytozome,[25] in turn, stores the assemblies and annotation files of dozen of plant genomes, also containing visualization and analysis tools 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Moreover, there is an interconnection between some databases in the information exchange/sharing and a major initiative was the International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration (INSDC)[26] which relates data from DDBJ,[27] EMBL-EBI,[28] and NCBI 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [29]
Nowadays, increase in size and complexity of molecular datasets leads to use of powerful statistical methods provided by computer science algorithms which are developed by machine learning area 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  Therefore, data mining and machine learning allow detection of patterns in data with a complex structure, as biological ones, by using methods of supervised and unsupervised learning, regression, detection of clusters and association rule mining, among others 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [23] To indicate some of them, self-organizing maps and k-means are examples of cluster algorithms; neural networks implementation and support vector machines models are examples of common machine learning algorithms 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Collaborative work among molecular biologists, bioinformaticians, statisticians and computer scientists is important to perform an experiment correctly, going from planning, passing through data generation and analysis, and ending with biological interpretation of the results 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence: [23]

Use of computationally intensive methods[edit]
On the other hand, the advent of modern computer technology and relatively cheap computing resources have enabled computer-intensive biostatistical methods like bootstrapping and re-sampling methods 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
In recent times, random forests have gained popularity as a method for performing statistical classification 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Random forest techniques generate a panel of decision trees 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Decision trees have the advantage that you can draw them and interpret them (even with a basic understanding of mathematics and statistics) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Random Forests have thus been used for clinical decision support systems 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [citation needed]

Applications[edit]
This section is in list format but may read better as prose 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  You can help by converting this section, if appropriate 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Editing help is available 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  (March 2016)
Public health[edit]
Public health, including epidemiology, health services research, nutrition, environmental health and health care policy & management 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In these medicine contents, it's important to consider the design and analysis of the clinical trials 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence:  As one example, there is the assessment of severity state of a patient with a prognosis of an outcome of a disease 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
With new technologies and genetics knowledge, biostatistics are now also used for Systems medicine, which consists in a more personalized medicine 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  For this, is made an integration of data from different sources, including conventional patient data, clinico-pathological parameters, molecular and genetic data as well as data generated by additional new-omics technologies 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [30]

Quantitative genetics[edit]
The study of population genetics and statistical genetics in order to link variation in genotype with a variation in phenotype 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In other words, it is desirable to discover the genetic basis of a measurable trait, a quantitative trait, that is under polygenic control 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  A genome region that is responsible for a continuous trait is called a quantitative trait locus (QTL) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The study of QTLs become feasible by using molecular markers and measuring traits in populations, but their mapping needs the obtaining of a population from an experimental crossing, like an F2 or recombinant inbred strains/lines (RILs) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  To scan for QTLs regions in a genome, a gene map based on linkage have to be built 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Some of the best-known QTL mapping algorithms are Interval Mapping, Composite Interval Mapping, and Multiple Interval Mapping 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [31]
However, QTL mapping resolution is impaired by the amount of recombination assayed, a problem for species in which it is difficult to obtain large offspring 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  Furthermore, allele diversity is restricted to individuals originated from contrasting parents, which limit studies of allele diversity when we have a panel of individuals representing a natural population 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [32] For this reason, the genome-wide association study was proposed in order to identify QTLs based on linkage disequilibrium, that is the non-random association between traits and molecular markers 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It was leveraged by the development of high-throughput SNP genotyping 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [33]
In animal and plant breeding, the use of markers in selection aiming for breeding, mainly the molecular ones, collaborated to the development of marker-assisted selection 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  While QTL mapping is limited due resolution, GWAS does not have enough power when rare variants of small effect that are also influenced by environment 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  So, the concept of Genomic Selection (GS) arises in order to use all molecular markers in the selection and allow the prediction of the performance of candidates in this selection 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The proposal is to genotype and phenotype a training population, develop a model that can obtain the genomic estimated breeding values (GEBVs) of individuals belonging to a genotype and but not phenotype population, called testing population 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [34] This kind of study could also include a validation population, thinking in the concept of cross-validation, in which the real phenotype results measured in this population are compared with the phenotype results based on the prediction, what used to check the accuracy of the model 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence: 
As a summary, some points about the application of quantitative genetics are:

This has been used in agriculture to improve crops (Plant breeding) and livestock (Animal breeding) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
In biomedical research, this work can assist in finding candidates gene alleles that can cause or influence predisposition to diseases in human genetics
Expression data[edit]
Studies for differential expression of genes from RNA-Seq data, as for RT-qPCR and microarrays, demands comparison of conditions 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The goal is to identify genes which have a significant change in abundance between different conditions 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  Then, experiments are designed appropriately, with replicates for each condition/treatment, randomization and blocking, when necessary 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Moderately Subjective 
 
Sentence:  In RNA-Seq, the quantification of expression uses the information of mapped reads that are summarized in some genetic unit, as exons that are part of a gene sequence 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  As microarray results can be approximated by a normal distribution, RNA-Seq counts data are better explained by other distributions 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  The first used distribution was the Poisson one, but it underestimate the sample error, leading to false positives 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Currently, biological variation is considered by methods that estimate a dispersion parameter of a negative binomial distribution 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Generalized linear models are used to perform the tests for statistical significance and as the number of genes is high, multiple tests correction have to be considered 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [35] Some examples of other analysis on genomics data comes from microarray or proteomics experiments 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [36][37] Often concerning diseases or disease stages 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [38]

Other studies[edit]
Ecology, ecological forecasting
Biological sequence analysis[39]
Systems biology for gene network inference or pathways analysis 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [40]
Clinical research and pharmaceutical development
Population dynamics, especially in regards to fisheries science 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence: 
Phylogenetics and evolution
Pharmacodynamics
Pharmacokinetics
Neuroimaging
Tools[edit]
There are a lot of tools that can be used to do statistical analysis in biological data 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Most of them are useful in other areas of knowledge, covering a large number of applications (alphabetical) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Here are brief descriptions of some of them:

ASReml: Another software developed by VSNi[41] that can be used also in R environment as a package 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is developed to estimate variance components under a general linear mixed model using restricted maximum likelihood (REML) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Models with fixed effects and random effects and nested or crossed ones are allowed 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Gives the possibility to investigate different variance-covariance matrix structures 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
CycDesigN:[42] A computer package developed by VSNi[41] that helps the researchers create experimental designs and analyze data coming from a design present in one of three classes handled by CycDesigN 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  These classes are resolvable, non-resolvable, partially replicated and crossover designs 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It includes less used designs the Latinized ones, as t-Latinized design 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [43]
Orange: A programming interface for high-level data processing, data mining and data visualization 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Include tools for gene expression and genomics 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [23]
R: An open source environment and programming language dedicated to statistical computing and graphics 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is an implementation of S language maintained by CRAN 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [44] In addition to its functions to read data tables, take descriptive statistics, develop and evaluate models, its repository contains packages developed by researchers around the world 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  This allows the development of functions written to deal with the statistical analysis of data that comes from specific applications 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [45] In the case of Bioinformatics, for example, there are packages located in the main repository (CRAN) and in others, as Bioconductor 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It is also possible to use packages under development that are shared in hosting-services as GitHub 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Highly Subjective 
 
Sentence: 
SAS: A data analysis software widely used, going through universities, services and industry 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Developed by a company with the same name (SAS Institute), it uses SAS language for programming 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
PLA 3 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 0:[46] Is a biostatistical analysis software for regulated environments (e 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: g 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  drug testing) which supports Quantitative Response Assays (Parallel-Line, Parallel-Logistics, Slope-Ratio) and Dichotomous Assays (Quantal Response, Binary Assays) 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  It also supports weighting methods for combination calculations and the automatic data aggregation of independent assay data 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
Weka: A Java software for machine learning and data mining, including tools and methods for visualization, clustering, regression, association rule, and classification 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  There are tools for cross-validation, bootstrapping and a module of algorithm comparison 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Weka also can be run in other programming languages as Perl or R 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence: [23]
Python (programming language) image analysis, deep-learning, machine-learning
SQL databases
NoSQL
NumPy numerical python
SciPy
SageMath
LAPACK linear algebra
MATLAB
Apache Hadoop
Apache Spark
Amazon Web Services
Scope and training programs[edit]
Almost all educational programmes in biostatistics are at postgraduate level 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  They are most often found in schools of public health, affiliated with schools of medicine, forestry, or agriculture, or as a focus of application in departments of statistics 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence: 
In the United States, where several universities have dedicated biostatistics departments, many other top-tier universities integrate biostatistics faculty into statistics or other departments, such as epidemiology 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  Thus, departments carrying the name "biostatistics" may exist under quite different structures 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  For instance, relatively new biostatistics departments have been founded with a focus on bioinformatics and computational biology, whereas older departments, typically affiliated with schools of public health, will have more traditional lines of research involving epidemiological studies and clinical trials as well as bioinformatics 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In larger universities around the world, where both a statistics and a biostatistics department exist, the degree of integration between the two departments may range from the bare minimum to very close collaboration 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Barely any subjectivity 
 
Sentence:  In general, the difference between a statistics program and a biostatistics program is twofold: (i) statistics departments will often host theoretical/methodological research which are less common in biostatistics programs and (ii) statistics departments have lines of research that may include biomedical applications but also other areas such as industry (quality control), business and economics and biological areas other than medicine 
Polarity: Neutral 
Subjectivity: Mild subjectivity 
 
Sentence:

See also: List of biostatistics journals
